tokens: I guess the next thing is to open the hatch, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got to get down out of 5 (psi suit pressure), too, here before I can turn too well and open the hatch.
I'm going to let it come down a little bit this time so I don't get down there unnecessarily.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's got a ways to go yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got a tone and it's (a) water tone (because he hasn't yet turned on his PLSS feedwater).
Okay, I'm going to go after that hatch.
(Pause) Can you slip to the right as far as you can?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Got to hold it (open) until the pressure decreases.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (looking out his window) All sorts of junk going out there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, get your (sublimator feed)water if you can.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like you to close Reg(ulator) A, please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's not an easy task (because the valve's behind me).
Gene, can you get my (PLSS feed)water?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, you got it open, so I need to turn around.
See if I can back in and (get) out of the way of the door.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What did you see in Reg A?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're seeing high suit pressures, stand by.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Challenger, Gene, you're Go to go out, and once you get out, maybe Jack can turn around and work on those a bit better.
We're seeing, I guess, the suit loop's a little high in pressure.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'm looking at about 4.7 on the suit loop right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What does it look like to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you're doing great; keep down.
Just a little hang-up on the DSKY.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I gotta get my arm down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You need to go to your left a little to clear the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This is one of those cards that...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you come forward just a little?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Come towards...(Come back) in the cabin just a little.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I'm on the porch, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, what do you want?
What can I do for you, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll get a word to you in one minute.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, in the...Well, I guess I ought to wait.
And everything looks normal on me, right now.
Wait 'til I get pressure down a little bit.
(Pause) Everything's normal, except a part of my nose itches I can't get to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll give you the jett(ison) bag anyway, Geno, while they're thinking.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I guess that's part of R&D (Research and Development).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like to have you stay in just a minute or so longer.
We're trying to keep track here of the suit circuit pressure and see if it stabilizes or starts to drop.
The one reg(ulator) has just has been intermittently leaking.
And we think we've got it shut off, but we're still watching it.
So bear with us just a minute or so.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I thought you isolated it last night.
Let me give you the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Give me that and I'll be on my way, working on the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Jack, and how about taking the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Suit Circuit Relief going Open, then Auto.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we'll watch it for a minute here and let you know.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And it's about 4:30 (on) a Wednesday afternoon, as I step out on to the plains of Taurus-Littrow.
The first thing I'll do is I'll turn the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'm very much interested in my Rover battery (temperature).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, you're Go for exit and looks like we've got it taken care of.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, and I'm checking the circuit breakers.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To himself) Okay, get the visor down, Geno.
Think it'd be better to leave it up.
We can look to the east for a change - a little bit, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Okay, I'll get the LCRU battery changed out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And as you walk by there, if you walk by in the right side of the Rover, how about giving us a SEP temperature readout, please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: 103, and the mirror is still clean.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let's see if I can change this little baby now.
(Pause) Bob, we have no use for the old (LCRU) battery, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm on the porch and the hatch is closed.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Are you talking to me or you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, that sounds familiar and looks familiar - the old plains.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Want me to get your antenna?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've already got the battery changed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Static ends) (Checking that the high-gain antenna is pointed at Earth There's the Earth right in the middle of the antenna (sighting scope).
Okay, verify (LCRU) Mode 3; I am in Mode 3; LCRU blankets are open 100 percent, battery covers I'm closing, the battery...Let me close it.
Yeah, you can come and get my
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't get close enough to you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, 17, if you guys are interested, your shadows will be 8 feet long tonight.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How many meters is that, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll draw it out...I'll step it out for ya.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Should I take my gloves off?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you leave them on for a while and see where we're going.
See what the boulder field looks like up there (at Station 6).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I know what it's going to look like.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The point is: my hands will be much better off without them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, battery covers are closed and tight.
Oh, they've even got TV, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Geno, when you push the Rover circuit breakers in, how about giving us a battery temperature reading on the Rover bats.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Tell them what my batteries are reading if I can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let me see if I can do something else while I'm waiting.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get the old SEP receiver.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, Bob, battery 1 is 95 degrees and battery 2 is reading zero.
So we got a gauge failure.
In fact, it's not reading zero; it's off scale low.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's a real cool-down, isn't it?
Okay, Jack, if you're going to worry about the SEP, standby and don't do the SEP until after you worry with the ETB, and we'll get to you on that.
When you get the ETB to the (CDR) seat, I'll talk to you about it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, we'll take the big bag.
I hope we can keep it on (the gate).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: A couple of things on that, Geno.
You might try tapping the thing (meaning the bag latch) to see if that loosens the dust.
There's also the hook business on the inside of the pallet that you could hook it (the big bag) on.
Caution: if you open the pallet, be careful not to knock the clamps off the fender.
But you can also reach over the pallet to put the big bag on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I brushed it and tapped it (the bag latch) yesterday.
I'm not sure we're going to have much luck with them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Trying to hang the big bag on the gate) I brushed them and tapped them yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You might want to put the big bag on the inside of the pallet there, if you can't operate them.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, mag Kilo goes on the 500; is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I've got Mary and Franny and Nancy (pause) and Donna (pause) and Bobbie and...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Now standing at Jack's right) Jack, I'm also going to keep this (the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Because it's too hard to get (the dustbrush off the front end).
We'll find a place for that in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Not convinced that this is a good thing to do) Well...Okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's just too hard to get off the front end.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Showing Cernan his checklist) That says the pallet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And if you open the pallet, be careful of the (fender) clamp.
Probably, if it's feasible, we suggest you reach across in front of the pallet.
Reach across the pallet to do it instead of opening it because of the clamps on the fender.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's not feasible to do that.
I got to open it, plus our hook is over center.
Let me get something to work on that with.
(Pause) You know, Bob, how that pallet locking hook can be out of the little C-shaped release in there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When you get done with the ETB, then you might save the grey tape out.
We're going to use a little bit of that on the SEP when you get done.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: When are you going to do that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What am I supposed to do, stand...Well...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll turn both switches on when you're out at the SEP transmitter.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, the tape is in the CDR's seat, and it'll still be there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, we'd like to take the tape from the CDR's seat and use it on the SEP, right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) You want me to do it or Gene to do it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you do it since the tape is there.
No, let's let Gene do it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Okay, Bob, the big bag is on the inside of the pallet.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The...(Talking to himself) And I know why.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is the thing that's in the way, Jack.
(To himself; discarding a thin, two-foot-long object) Get rid of this thing.
(To Bob) Okay, opening and closing of the pallet didn't interfere at all with those fenders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The big bag is on the inside (of the geopallet), though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, but it's also in the way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Want me to get out of the way?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It drags over that locking device.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me just see what we got to do here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, are you going out to take the pan now?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, as soon as I finish up here, I'll do that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And after you take the pan, we'd like you to retrieve the cosmic ray experiment.
They're expecting a little solar storm, and before the rain gets on the cosmic ray experiment, they'd like to retrieve it.
We'll leave it in the ETB during the traverse.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, (I'll do it) after the pan.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It will just be a nominal retrieval.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...and we'll put it in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Long Pause) SCB-7; 20-bag dispenser goes on my camera when it gets back.
Short can under the LMP's seat.
Jack, I'll just go ahead and mount some of these bags on your camera while I'm here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, if you got time there with the camera...when you get done with the camera...how about getting some grey tape and we'll put you to work on SEP for about a minute.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me finish with SCB-7 here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And did you get Jack's camera fixed last night?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Going to the gate with the core cap dispenser) Yeah, we did.
(Pause; returning to Jack's seat with the core caps) Okay, there is already one (core-cap dispenser) on the gate.
Leave that one there (in Jack's seat pan).
Okay, (reading quickly) SCB-7 to gate; 20-bag dispenser on commander's camera.
We'll do it when he (Jack) gets back (with the camera); 20 bags on the LMP's camera; core cap dispenser to gate.
There's one there (on the gate), there's one under the (LMP) seat.
Short can's under the LMP's seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think 8 went in (to the cabin after EVA-2 with SCB-6).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We'll put either 4 or 5 on there.
I'll have to wait until he gets back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Fender wrinkled up in the Sun a little bit last night.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Bob, I took two 5-foot stereopairs of the (cosmic ray) configuration.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we'll stick it in the ETB and just hang it there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And in case you're wondering, and so you don't confuse it with a rock, it's in (sample) bag 106.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What do you want done to the SEP?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Take some grey tape over to the receiver, Gene.

And with reference to the fact that there is some Velcro missing on the front there which hold the covers down, we'd like to tape the two covers together in the middle there - you know, where the two sides overlap in the middle of the box.
A short piece about an inch long should do it if they are clean.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I doubt if the tape will stick because it doesn't (stick) on dust, but I might be able to go over it with one piece to clean it and another piece to tape it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the question beyond that: is there Velcro to hold one of those flaps down or not?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, so the Velcro is missing from both flaps, I take it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, what happened was that the tape (means "adhesive") that held the lower Velcro on there apparently came loose, and it stuck to the upper Velcro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: In that case, we'd like to take a piece of tape and tape the cover down to keep it closed when it's supposed to be closed.

The feeling is that if the cover flaps partly open, you may get specular reflection off the inside of the Mylar down onto the mirrors causing it to heat up during the drive when it's supposed to be closed.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, we'll give it a try.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And, Jack, if you're done, you might go rescue EP number 5 from the (LM) footpad, and we'll put it under the LMP's seat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, there are a lot of other things under there.
I'll rescue it; we'll see where the best place to put it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (At the gate, to Gene) Hey, I got bags on you.
I got bags on your camera, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we're going to put those two bags on the rear there on our PLSSs?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The one under the LMP's seat will go on the CDR, it's the one with all the stuff in it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I got core tubes in 7 here, Jack.
We'll put either one of those...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So I can put the charge under my seat.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's affirm, I think, Jack; once you get SCB-7 out of there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Taping the SEP) I feel like a kid stuck in taffy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sure is strange not to see some fine-grained rocks out here.
Seen a couple, but certainly not very many.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That rock that you picked up at ra(ndom)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Noticing the TV pointing into the sky) What are you doing up there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Realizing that the TV is pointed at Earth) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I hope it solves the problem.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, probably not any more than we would like to see it solved.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Gene, your bag's going to have two lowers and one upper (drive tube).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you re-sort things there, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Did you re-sort things in SCB-7?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bag 7's got...(Stops to listen) Bob, I...(Pause) Go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Our understanding was there were two uppers and one lower in bag 7, and two lowers under the LMP seat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I just wanted to make sure that we know what you are so we don't let you get away too far (from the Rover) with two uppers and a lower.
Two lowers and an upper is certainly better than two uppers and a lower.
As long as we know what it is.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's two lowers and an upper.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you're ready, I'll configure you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, here, let me get this on you first since I got...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I'm going to ask you to turn a 180 degrees because you're up on a hill.
I'll never be able to do it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm down in a hole now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) Tallest man on the Moon right now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm going to put SCB-4 on Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Gets corrected by somebody in Houston) SCB-4; copy.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, I got to get these PLSS straps, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I saw them as you got out, and then I forget about them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, yours is on over here.
Probably a better time to do them, anyway, rather than when we go out.
Okay, let me get the bag.
I'll get the other one when I configure your other side.
Okay, want to get my PLSS straps?
Then I'll be cleaned up, and then I finish your other side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get the other one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, stay right where you are so I can get this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now come over here, and I'll get you a core-cap dispenser, which I left here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (At his seat) (Seismic charge number) 5's under my seat.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got 5 there; we got (charges) 2 and 3 on the Rover; LCRU blankets are open 100 percent; battery covers are closed.
I want to...(To Jack) Push that battery cover over there down just to make sure it goes down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Push it (the battery cover) on down over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll take a look at that gauge again, but the gauge on the high battery looked like it may have failed.
Okay, LCRU blankets are open, battery covers are closed and pushed closed, dust LCRU...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Carrying the scoop) I'm going to the SEP (transmitter).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait a minute before you do.
Just come over here by the left front wheel.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I know you got a second.
Just a little bit closer to the left front wheel, towards me.
Ah; that's good, anywhere in there.
(Pause, taking Jack's picture) Wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you do that likewise (and take my picture)?
Or can you hold it with that other camera?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you might want to take a couple...(Long Pause; static, even over Bob)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We think somebody lost their comm.
Jack, it's probably Gene going to zero.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How do you read me, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just jiggled my Mode switch here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We understand TV stowed and you're taken care of in the comm.
And you might give us a Rover readout either now or when you get to the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay; we'll see which is convenient.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (At the) SEP is probably more convenient while you're sitting there waiting for the Nav to warm up or initialize - waiting for us to give you the reading.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, are you watching LMP?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Not any more he (meaning Ed Fendell) isn't.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I just wondered because I just took the TV.
I just want to make sure we got comm here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we're reading you in Mode 1.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, for your information, we've both got our cover gloves off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, that's in, that's in, that's in.
Should have dusted my checklist on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the old tape fix on the SEP (solar panel)'s still working.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Both mirrors (means solar panels) have a little angular displacement, but not more than 5 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Sounds like that's the least of the SEP's problem, but we have hope.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene, who is orienting the Rover for a Navigation initialization) Okay, you're going to be over there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That roll indicator isn't worth a ding-dong.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Roll zero, pitch is zero; heading is 291; distance, 001; range, 000; amps hours are 90 and 85; volts are 65 (and) 65; Sun shaft device, by the way, is 0.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Batteries are 100 and off-scale low, and motors are all off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and, Gene, we'd like to torque (the heading indicator) to two-eight-seven.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's the heading from Cross City to Tyndall (Air Force Base).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, 45 Yankee is a sample from near the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We copied 45 Yankee near the SEP.

If you give us a frame count when you get done, and give us an approximate location for the Rover, at least crosswise from the Y (meaning from the intersection of the SEP transmitter wires), we'd appreciate it.
And we also need SEP receiver power and DSEA both on.
And we'd like the (SEP receiver) cover taped down when you get done, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, keep me honest on those reels.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get over on the reel.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did I miss this other reel?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What about the one coming west?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's what I...No, you're okay on the one west.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You want to...Head towards the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Head towards it and then make your turn (to the north).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Matter of fact, turn on these tracks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, that 45 Yankee (sample) was a fine-grained basalt, I think.
One of the few around here.
That's why I picked it up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm stopped and I'm ready to go.
(To Bob) I'm 2 meters to the west of the north line.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I guess I'm certainly within 5 meters of the transmitter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we'll get that in the photos.
And Gene, how's the low-gain oriented?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It (the low-gain antenna)'s oriented 355 and my (Rover) heading is 352.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you want the (SEP) receiver on...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Both the receiver and the recorder on, both switches on and then tape the cover down.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) I don't know if that tape is going to hold.
(Pause) Okay, it's taped down more or less.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And then I guess I'm supposed to get on, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, Nav Reset is now Off and I'm all zeroed up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we're ready for you guys to roll.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, what's the first range and bearing to the Rover sample, past Jones?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, it will be 185 (on the heading) and 1.5 on the range.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) I've forgotten how (to get on the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, that Challenger looks pretty from here, you know it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 185 and 1.5 and I'm going to head on about 012.
We ought to go right through Jones.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and, Gene, remember the driving fairly slow - or fairly well controlled - the first 300 meters, and a "mark" at the end of the antenna.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Watch that, Jack, watch that antenna lead...Uh-oh.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (What does it) look like to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll pick up that same spot, I can see right where I was.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Give us another mark when you start up on that side.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We'll give you a hack, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I'm right on the track.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's exactly...I just came right over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We can't go too far in this heading.
We've got a big hole up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let's see, this is east...Looks awf(ul)...It's a double crater but it's much bigger than I thought Rudolph would be.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, if you're where you think you are, you're east of Rudolph quite a ways.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gee, I think you ought to know where we are by now, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: After you give me a mark there, I'll talk to you about it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I guess you didn't hear it.
We're passed the end of the antenna and we're headed northeast.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you give me a mark when you started or a mark when you passed the antenna?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I gave you a mark when I started and it took about 20 seconds to get to the end.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Is that good enough or do you want me to go back?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, if you look at your contour map there, we think you are located right now at approximately where the P in SEP is, just below the P in "Poppy".
(Note that the map has the crater name mis-spelled.) In which case you're probably driving through that little crater that's just to the northeast there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's probably the one you came upon.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, I wish I could see a little bit better.
(Pause) The major boulders still look like the pyroxene gabbro.
The surface texture has not changed.
There is a granule population, now that I look at it more closely with the shadows.

(That is, now that they are driving north, he is noticing the larger granules on the surface because of the shadows they cast.) But I have a feeling that most of those are...They look like they're just very small clods.
That should show up in some of the bulk samples we've taken.
But, it is remarkable to me...the small number of fine-grain rocks.
There's one at about halfway between the SEP and the LM that I'd like to pick up, it's a fairly good sized one.
Maybe we can get it when we get back.
It looks like a fine-grained basalt.
(Pause) I may have sampled one in 45 Yankee there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I tell you, it's not exactly the greatest place to navigate through.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you ought to bear left, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I just want to get across this...around these boulders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's a crater we're just passing at 207/0.4 about 20 meters in diameter, with the pyroxene gabbro blocks on the rim, a few of them.

It's not an exceptionally blocky rim crater, but we are in an area where the block population is up to about 5 percent (coverage of the surface), in contrast to most of the area we traversed yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I tell you, going is a little bit rough; there's a population of blocks as Jack said and an awful lot of small craters.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I was just going to add that the frequency of craters in the 10-meter size range is quite a bit higher than we were used to yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) (To Bob) And they all - although not exceptionally blocky rim - they all have a slightly, maybe 2 or 3 or 5 percent more blocks in their walls and on their rim than does the normal terrain.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Still no obvious structure within the dark mantling material itself.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, they changed it on us.
Still seeing the little pit-bottom craters with the glass in them.
I've forgotten the acronym already, Bob, I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And you asked me for an LMP frame count a while back and I believe it was 5.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That was after the SEP photos, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Negative; that was before the SEP photos.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, looking up at the North Massif, we see the scattered, strewn field of boulders that generally seem to start, more or less, from a line of large boulders, which might indicate some structure.
And those lines are roughly horizontal across the face that we're looking at.
The boulder tracks are irregular in shape, obviously downhill, but you'll see in the pictures that they are (pause) curved in places.
But they're all - that I see - tend to be aggregates of little craters where the boulder was obviously tumbling and bouncing a little bit.
(Pause) We're out in (a) population of fragments now in the immediate area, at...Is that 188?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (The population's) generally about 1 percent between craters.
But at the crater rims, it's up to about 5 percent.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And how far down the North Massif...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Listens to Bob) Oh, there are several of them, Bob.
What I'm talking about is about 100-meter-long lines where the boulder trains initiate.
And there's one (that) looks like about halfway, maybe two-thirds of the way down in perspective.
Another one that's probably about halfway.
They're just sort of scattered around on the Massif.
(Pause) I think we're getting close to...Well, we couldn't be.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got to move over here a little.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, our heading that they're sending us down here, it really should put us to west of Jones.
(Pause) A lot of static in the background today.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I think we are talking to you guys through the LM right now; and how about a speed reading?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're at 12 clicks and we're full bore.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I wish I could give you more on that structure in there (that is, structure in the North Massif), but I think those lines of boulder sources are about all we can see right now.
Talked about the lineaments yesterday and they're not nearly as obvious today in the higher Sun.
Looking up Wessex Cleft, even with the Sun in the flat area there, it looks darker than the North Massif side.
But again, the Sun angle may be fooling us, but I recall it was darker on the (overhead) photos.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The old man wrinkled face on the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...Sculptured Hills, though, is evident as soon as you come out of the Wessex Cleft.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And they look like there are boulders up on the side of Sculptured Hills; except that they aren't nearly as big as those on the North Massif.
The areas where the boulder source is look like they're made up of boulders no bigger than a meter maybe; whereas, the North Massif boulders are up to several meters.
Those boulder sources all seem to be up within a third of the height of the Sculptured Hills, just east of the Wessex Cleft.
Here is a boulder track that crossed the slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It looks like it goes, rather than perpendicular to the contours, it probably is crossing them in a fairly straight line on an angle of 60 degrees, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That one may be fairly near...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, see that big boulder with that big track?
It looks like it's an elongated, rolled-up boulder.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like it may be broken now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, is this a Rover sample?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Tell me where you want it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...see that little pit right over there about 30 feet ahead.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I've got two pictures there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay, this is a soil sample.
(Pause; waiting for Gene to finish taking his "locator", then calling his attention to the waiting sample) Hey, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and I just took a locator (probably a picture of the horizon, in this case); and CDR is on frame 41.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your bag (the SCB on the accessory staff) open?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We ought to tape that lead down if we can remember it next stop.
It's in the way of...It's sticking up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That thing came off that piece of Velcro.
I'll get it when I get back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And LMP's frame count (pause) is three-five.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'd like a bearing and range.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bearing and range for the large block, just beyond...Let's see; it's just beyond the crater Henry.
The large block there near the break of the slope, which is our next aiming point.
The bearing and range there is 188 and 2.8.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, what do you see in the way of boulders coming down the base of the Sculptured Hills, in terms of sampling opportunities at Station 8 and in terms of any boulder tracks that might lead down to boulders that might just possibly be accessible at Station 8?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Bob) Boulder tracks are not obvious on Sculptured Hills at all.
It looks like there are fragments over there that would have had their sources higher up the slope.
I think we can get boulders there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll have to get a little closer, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll find out in a couple of hours.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I will give you a reading on that before long.
I wouldn't eliminate Station 8 for the world - or the Moon - whatever's available today.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See that big boulder, Jack, with those tracks?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks like it may have stopped rolling because it broke up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, they've got the low-gain right on 'em.
But, I tell you, we still got static.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't like the sound of your bounces.
Okay, you've got yourself in some holes here.
(Pause) You've never...I've read you all along, though, so there's no problem.
(Pause) Looks like you're getting up on the rim of Henry here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Mulling it over) Well...No, Henry should be to...I should be well west of Henry, I think.
I wouldn't be surprised if Henry isn't right over that little rise on the right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the surface structure hasn't changed ...(correcting himself) texture (hasn't changed).
We're on a little bit of a rise in here now and still about 1 percent of the surface...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, how about a range and bearing.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I thought you were close to Henry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we're just southwest of Henry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's called a wheel, I think.
(Pause) And Henry looks much like Horatio did.
Has boulders on its inner wall (but) not as many.
They look light colored: a light-albedo, gabbroic appearance.
There may be some right down there, though, that are fine grained; they look a little greyer.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, there's our target...There's...Either one of...That's one right down there on (garbled) break in slope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See the one we've got over has a boulder track.
That's the one, that crossed slope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, if we could get up...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's Station 6, and that was the...(naming the rock) the turning boulder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Station 6: we can probably get up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we can; it doesn't look too bad.
(To Bob) The break in slope, right now, doesn't show anything obvious, except that's where the boulders start.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we hope that's fairly obvious.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And on up the hill you have...(Stops to listen to Bob) But as I was saying, Henry just looks like a somewhat more mantled Horatio.
(Laughs, then makes an aside) (This dialog is) getting to be ridiculous.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, Bob, I'm navigating - headed northwest now - to get around the western rim of Henry.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And on that west rim, we've got about 10 percent boulder cover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And a reminder, Jack, to keep taking your Rover photos.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And by "boulder", I generally mean "fragment", Bob, in this case.
When I say 10 percent, I'm looking at stuff greater than about a centimeter in diameter.
(Pause) I'll try to say "fragment" from now on and be more precise.
Here's a little area where there's...This is the one part of the rim of Henry I see that has fairly large fragments, or boulders, on them up to 2 or 3 meters.
But, again, they all appear to be buried.
There are very few - except small ones - sitting out on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, you know, the fragment population out here only goes out maybe 200 meters, I expect.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now this (particular) concentration of boulders (we are driving past) is because of a 50-meter crater in the rim of Henry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that was one that we...(Hears Bob)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Take a picture in here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, that's not...Locke's right ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is one (of) about 50 meters (diameter) right on the rim crest of Henry, the due-west rim.
Now Locke is just ahead of us.
It also has boulders in its walls but has relatively few on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Characteristic of both Henry, Locke, and Horatio is essentially no change in the average frequency of boulders on the rim.
The increase comes in the wall.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're just about between Henry and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you guys are heading for that big boulder, which must be just dead ahead of you there, about half a kilometer.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, Gene's sort of headed for Station 6 now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to take a tour around that boulder and give them a fix on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, and that would be a good mark to give us a range and bearing on, since it's a pretty discrete point.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, the boulder concentrations in the wall of Henry have their upslope start (that is, upper limit) at about, oh, I would guess an average of 30 meters down from the rim crest.
The rim crest of Henry is not very well defined, but it's there.
And from that initiation of boulders, they stream down the slope to the break-in-slope down at the floor.
Still no obvious change in the dark mantle, as we're just to the east of Locke now.
(Pause) There's a 30-meter crater, fairly subdued but still quite deep...(That is, with a) subdued rim.
Again, it looks as if it were mantled; (and it) has no significant increase in blocks on its rim.
That crater, in any other place, would have been a very blocky-rim crater.
It's maybe 30 meters (across) by 5 meters deep.
Man, that is a big rock up there!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Turning Point Rock is a split rock; (it) has (what) looks like a northwest-southeast overhang, with another block just this side of it, just to the south of that overhang.
It's a pyramid shape in cross section - triangular shape in cross section - and it looks like it is pretty well fractured, although not pervasively like the rock at Shorty was.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Jack, I know I can get up to Station 6.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, Bob, Station 6 rock - one of them - is from that boulder track that runs obliquely across the contours.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the pictures ought to pin down at least the (upper) end of the boulder track pretty well.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, this is a big rock, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As I saw it, the boulder track stopped about halfway up the slope of the North Massif.
(To Gene) That (Turning Point Rock) is a big rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it looks like it's - I don't know if it's mantled on top, but it's certainly filleted.
There's a lot of the dark mantle up and on some of the shallower slopes of the boulder.
And it's on a little mound itself, as if much of it might be covered up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks like a breccia from here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you get a sample of it right here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm 3 meters from Turning Point Rock on the east side, and I'm reading 186 and 2.8.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's see...No, I can get them.
The thing is, I don't know what it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, but it's part of these fragments around here.
(Pause) I guess Turning Point Rock is (visual measuring the rock) 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6...Six meters high anyway.
It's a...Well, I'd say it's a very rough, subrounded type of rock.
By the face...Let me get this, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There are two fragments in that sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it (the sample location)'s about 4 meters from Turning Point Rock on the north side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And presume you got some good photos of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'll tell you what I'm going to do here, real quick.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, let me spin around this little crater here to the left.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, it's very coarsely vesicular; but, at first glance, it did not look like the pyroxene gabbro - although that rock (another rock nearby) does.
It looks like it might be fragmental, although I'm suspicious that I'm looking at zap pits.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (I'm) getting them (that is, the black&white pictures).
(To Gene) Take one (that is, a color picture).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, we're on a little rise looking at this boulder (from the north).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) You know that...(To Bob) Bob, my guess is, right now, is that Turning Point Rock is a big piece of subfloor gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I gather you changed your opinion.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: What looked like fragments is just big spalls where the zap pits have cleaned off the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, guys, you might be happy to know that we think we've finally found the LM, because we were calling that for 188 and 2.8, and you got there at 186 and 2.8.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It (the Station 6 boulder)'s the split one up there, Jack.
I've had my eye on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's some big boulders down here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I sort of lost track of Station 6.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've had my eye on that boulder.
You can't see the track from here.
We'll be looking right up it; looking right up the old boulder track.
(Pause) Man, I tell you, this navigating through here is not...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're in a region where, really, the general fragment population is no different.
We're up off the break in slope, although you wouldn't notice it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But we are, quite a ways.
But the fragment population is not much different than out on the plains.
The big difference is that there are these scattered blocks that are from a meter to probably 10 meters...(Correcting himself) No, 5 meters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll be looking right up that track.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I didn't realize you were (chuckling) that far upslope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Not very uncomfortable for me on this side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just...Until I looked down here and saw the slope we're on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I can't see any obvious change in albedo, like we could see with the light mantle yesterday.
(Pause) (To Gene) You got a...Don't...There you got her: a nice, nice place.
Oh, oh, you don't want to go over that way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, 17, you want to park at a heading of 107; we're going to open the battery covers and let them cool at this station.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, that's going to be moderately level right there (on the west side of the boulder).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Trouble is, they (Houston)'re looking into the shady side of the block.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, if I park on the other side, they won't be able to...I can go right upslope a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I can't go up there.
Let me just...This is going to have to be good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I think you're all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's not very level, but we're not going to get much more level than that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) That's the best I can do.
That's not very level for the gravimeter, but...Let me see if I can get comm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
